next_steps: Test stool for Clostridium botulinum toxin and culture
Administer botulism immune globulin (BIG-IV) as soon as suspected
Maintain strict respiratory monitoring and mechanical ventilation support
Avoid aminoglycoside antibiotics which can worsen neuromuscular blockade
Stool sample testing for Clostridium botulinum toxin and culture
Early administration of Botulism Immune Globulin Intravenous (BIG-IV) if infant botulism is highly suspected
Electromyography (EMG) with repetitive nerve stimulation to evaluate neuromuscular transmission
Strict avoidance of aminoglycosides and other medications that may exacerbate NMJ blockade
Consultation with pediatric neurology and ophthalmology for detailed assessment of cranial nerve function
Obtain serum toxicology screen, including heavy metals, and serum thiamine levels
Continue intensive cardiorespiratory monitoring and mechanical ventilation support
